questionHtml:
The standard electrode potential of zinc can be measured using a standard hydrogen electrode (SHE).

Draw and annotate the diagram to show the complete apparatus required to measure the standard electrode potential of zinc.
markschemeHtml:
H2 (g) entering at «298 K and» 100 kPa ✔

platinum electrode on left ✔

voltmeter connecting electrodes AND salt bridge connecting electrolytes ✔

1 mol dm–3 H+ on the left AND 1 mol dm–3 Zn2+ on the right ✔

 

Voltmeter and salt bridge need to be drawn but not necessarily annotated for M3.

Concentrations, but not state symbols, required for M4.

meta.title: The standard electrode potential of zinc can be measured using a standard hydrogen electrode (SHE). Draw and annotate the diagram to show the complete apparatus required to measure
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.2.12—The hydrogen half-cell H+(aq) + e− ⇌12 H2(g) is assigned a standard electrode potential of zero by convention. It is used in the measurement of standard electrode potential, E⦵. Interpret standard electrode potential data in terms of ease of oxidation/reduction.
